opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't walk straight, my brain is completely blank, and my feet are burning like fire.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last couple of days I've gone really unsteady on my feet, my thinking is foggy, and my feet are burning — I nearly can't walk and I'm scared I'll fall.

symptom_narrative.onset: it came on over about two days, I think after I ate something with hidden gluten
symptom_narrative.character: my balance is all over the place and my feet burn like fire
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe — I can't walk safely without two people holding me
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my balance/whole body for the unsteadiness, and both feet for the burning
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening over the two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting or lying still, and holding onto someone
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to stand or walk, and the dark
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my head feels completely blank and slow, my feet and lower legs feel numb and burning, and I'm terrified of falling
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had milder versions with gluten before, but never this bad or this unsteady
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I need two people to help me walk to the toilet safely

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: my husband is here but he can't take my weight if I start to fall, and we've got loose rugs and the bathroom is poorly lit
